item.title: VIEW S&P 500, Nasdaq notch fresh records, casting aside war fears
item.description: U.S. stock indexes returned to new highs on Wednesday, putting fears of economic disruption driven by the war with Iran in the rearview mirror.

item.title: Ford's advanced EV-and-tech chief to leave the company
item.description: Doug Field, a Tesla and ​Apple veteran who has led Ford Motor's electric-vehicle and technology efforts for nearly five years, is leaving ‌the automaker.

item.title: IMF says Bank of Japan can see through inflationary shock from Iran war
item.description: The Bank of Japan can see through inflationary pressures from the war in the Middle East as any second-round effects on broader prices will be limited, Rahul Anand, the International Monetary Fund's mission chief for Japan, told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: Bessent supports IMF quota revamp, wants World Bank financing for critical minerals
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Wednesday reaffirmed his support for a long-delayed revamp of the International Monetary Fund's quota resources and called on the World Bank to move quickly to support projects to develop critical ​minerals to diversify supplies away from China.

item.title: Permian-focused EagleRock plans rare US oil and gas IPO, sources say
item.description: EagleRock, a company that collects royalties and fees from oil and gas ‌production on the land it controls in the Permian Basin, is exploring a U.S. initial public offering that could value it at up to $2 billion, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: NY jury finds Live Nation illegally monopolized ticketing markets, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Live Nation illegally monopolized ​ticketing markets, a New ‌York federal jury found Wednesday, Bloomberg News reported.

item.title: Exclusive: Fed's Musalem says oil shock likely to keep core inflation near 3%, rates on hold for some time
item.description: High oil prices are likely to keep underlying inflation nearly a percentage point above the Federal Reserve's ​2% target for the rest of this year, with the U.S. central bank likely needing to leave interest rates unchanged, St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem said on Wednesday.

item.title: Sazerac offers to buy Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman for $15 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: U.S. spirits group Sazerac has offered to buy Jack Daniel's ​maker Brown-Forman for about $15 billion, the ‌Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US probes suspicious oil trades made before Trump Iran pivots, source says
item.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission is investigating ​a series of oil futures trades that were placed ‌shortly before major policy shifts by U.S. President Donald Trump related to the war in Iran, a person familiar with the matter said on Wednesday .

item.title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial's early investors won’t be able to fully cash out until after Trump’s term
item.description: World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture co-founded ​by President Trump and his sons, released a new proposal Wednesday that would prevent early investors from trading tokens – 80% of their holdings ‌are currently locked by the firm – for two years, followed by an additional two-year vesting period, according to a statement posted on its governance forum.

item.title: Exclusive: European buyers hold talks to ship Canadian LNG via Panama Canal to diversify supply
item.description: European buyers, including Germany's Uniper, are exploring the possibility of purchasing liquefied ​natural gas from Canada's Pacific coast and shipping it through the Panama Canal as part of a long-term strategy to diversify supply, ‌an effort made more urgent in the wake of the Iran war, two sources familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Iran war puts US companies into wait-and-see mode, Fed report says
item.description: U.S. economic activity increased and employment was steady in recent weeks, the ​Federal Reserve said on Wednesday in a report that also pointed to notable impacts from a surge ‌in energy prices amid the ongoing Iran war.

item.title: White House has no opinion on potential United Airlines deal for American Airlines
item.description: The Trump administration does not have an opinion on reports ​that United Airlines was considering merging ‌with rival American Airlines , White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters on Wednesday.

item.title: Bessent says he is optimistic Warsh will be Fed chair 'on time'
item.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott ‌Bessent on Wednesday said he was optimistic that Kevin ​Warsh, President Donald ​Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve ⁠Chair, would assume ​the post on time.

item.title: BoE's Bailey says progress on global stablecoin standards has slowed
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said on Wednesday that progress on establishing ​international standards for so-called stablecoins, cryptoassets which ‌are pegged to a fiat currency such as the dollar, had slowed over the last year.

item.title: S&P 500 hits first intraday record high since US-Iran war
item.description: The S&P 500 touched an intraday record high on Wednesday, its first since the U.S.-Iran conflict began, as hopes of a de-escalation in the war ​and robust earnings expectations drew investors back into risk assets.

item.title: Morgan Stanley eyes capital relief as lobbying push pays off
item.description: Morgan Stanley's capital requirements will remain ​flat or fall modestly under an overhaul of the U.S. capital regime, its Chief Financial Officer Sharon Yeshaya told Reuters, a ‌win for the Wall Street bank that has pushed hard for reforms.

item.title: Exclusive: Two Sigma, D.E. Shaw join Wall Street push against US SEC's bid to relax quarterly reporting
item.description: Two Sigma Investments and D.E. Shaw are among several Wall Street investment firms and hedge funds that are fighting a proposal to allow companies to opt out of ​quarterly reporting, according to people familiar with the matter, on concerns it would reduce the flow of key financial information for investors in public companies.

item.title: Cadence, Nvidia working together on developing AI for robotics
item.description: Cadence Design Systems ​and Nvidia are partnering to further ‌the development of artificial intelligence for robots, the CEOs of the two companies said on Wednesday.

item.title: Europe sees record jet inflows from US in April
item.description: Europe was seeing record inflows of jet fuel from the United States, data from Kpler and LSEG showed, as the region attempts to shore up fuel supplies to make up for the disruption ​of imports from the Middle East.

item.title: Wall Street still betting on 2026 deal boom, but Middle East unrest adds caution
item.description: Wall Street's biggest banks still expect 2026 to be a strong year for dealmaking, although turmoil in the Middle East ​that has roiled global markets has tempered executive optimism.

item.title: L3Harris announces $1.27 billion expansion project at Virginia site
item.description: L3Harris Technologies and Virginia Governor Abigail Spanberger said on Wednesday the defense supplier will ​invest $1.27 billion to further the solid rocket ‌motor production capacity at its Orange County site.

item.title: Tax enforcement weakened after Trump job cuts, IRS data shows
item.description: The Trump administration dramatically reduced efforts to pursue tax cheats in 2025, ​government data shows, shedding tens of thousands of employees at the Internal Revenue Service, including those charged with ‌enforcement.

item.title: Robinhood, Webull jump after US SEC approves removal of day-trading limit for smaller investors
item.description: Shares of retail trading platforms Robinhood and Webull surged on Wednesday after the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission paved the way for a new regulatory ​framework on day-trading limit for smaller investors.

item.title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
item.description: European Central Bank supervisors are set to warn bankers ​about the risks posed by ‌Anthropic's new artificial intelligence model that might supercharge cyberattacks, one source familiar ​with the situation told Reuters.

item.title: Allbirds shares jump over 400% on plans to pivot to AI from sneakers
item.description: Shares of Allbirds surged more than five-fold on Wednesday after the footwear maker said it was raising capital and pivoting towards AI computing infrastructure.

item.title: Big ad agencies settle US FTC probe into alleged boycott over political content
item.description: Three large advertising agencies ​settled a U.S. Federal Trade Commission probe accusing them of violating ‌antitrust law by conspiring to boycott online media platforms based on political content they didn't like, the agency said on Wednesday.

item.title: Middle East war damage to energy assets may cost up to $58 billion, research firm Rystad says
item.description: The Middle East conflict could saddle the region with as much as $58 billion in repair costs for energy-linked infrastructure, with oil ​and gas facilities alone accounting for up to $50 billion, according to ‌a report by Rystad Energy.

item.title: BYD shuns price war in South Africa to build EV brand
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD ‌is deliberately avoiding a price war in South Africa as competition ramps up in the continent's second biggest auto market, its local managing director told Reuters on Wednesday.

item.title: EU warns Meta WhatsApp AI fee breaches antitrust rules, orders rollback
item.description: The European Commission said on Wednesday it intended to order Meta Platforms to reinstate rival artificial intelligence assistants on its WhatsApp messaging service after the U.S. ​tech giant imposed an access fee.
